skill.name: image-ocr
skill.description: Extract text from images with Tesseract OCR. Supports PNG, JPEG, TIFF, BMP images. Select a language model or run OCR via natural-language requests.
skill.summary: Extract text from images using Tesseract OCR with multi-language support and natural-language path parsing.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static scanner flagged 75 findings with risk score 100/100. Manual evaluation confirms 100% false positive rate for all HIGH, CRITICAL, and MEDIUM findings. The 41 Ruby/shell backtick findings are Markdown code formatting backticks in documentation, not shell execution. The 8 env_access findings are JSON config file reads (config.get), not environment variable access. The 8 weak cryptographic algorithm findings are Windows file path examples and documentation text, not cryptography. The CRITICAL heuristic combination finding aggregates these false positives. The only confirmed risk factors are external_commands (Tesseract via pytesseract, required for OCR) and network (sample image download from official tesseract-ocr GitHub repository), both legitimate and expected for this OCR utility. No malicious intent, data exfiltration, credential theft, or obfuscation detected.

content.user_title: Extract Text from Images Using OCR
content.value_statement: Manually transcribing text from images is slow and error-prone. This skill uses Tesseract OCR to automatically extract text from image files with multi-language support.
actual_capabilities: Extract text from PNG, JPEG, TIFF, BMP, GIF, and WebP image formats
Support multiple Tesseract language models including English and Chinese
Parse natural-language requests to automatically locate image file paths
Configure OCR execution through a JSON configuration file
Run OCR from the command line using a single Python script
Download and process sample images for quick testing and verification
limitations: Requires Tesseract OCR to be installed separately on the system
OCR accuracy depends on image quality and may require preprocessing
Limited to languages with installed Tesseract language packs
Cannot process scanned PDFs without first converting to images

use_cases.title: Digitize Scanned Research Documents
use_cases.description: Convert scanned academic papers and research documents into editable text for literature review and analysis.

use_cases.title: Automate Document Processing Workflow
use_cases.description: Integrate OCR into data processing pipelines to extract text from images automatically.

use_cases.title: Extract Text from Screenshots and Photos
use_cases.description: Quickly capture text from screenshots, photographed documents, or presentation slides.

prompt_templates.title: Basic OCR Text Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all text from the image at [image_path] using OCR.

prompt_templates.title: OCR with Language Selection
prompt_templates.prompt: Run OCR on [image_path] using the [lang] language model and return the recognized text.

prompt_templates.title: OCR with Result Validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract text from [image_path], run OCR with language [lang], validate the output for completeness, and report any recognition issues.

prompt_templates.title: Batch OCR Processing Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: For all images in [directory_path], run Tesseract OCR with language [lang], combine extracted text into a single document, and flag any images where OCR failed or returned empty results.

output_examples.input: Extract text from the scanned research paper at images/paper_page_01.png
output_examples.output: Abstract: This study examines the effects of machine learning on diagnostic accuracy in medical imaging. The analysis included 1,247 patient records across three clinical sites.

output_examples.input: Run OCR on the photograph at photos/sign.jpg using the English language model
output_examples.output:
Welcome to Central Hospital
Outpatient Entrance
Hours: 8:00 AM - 8:00 PM
Emergency: 24 hours

用户协议
第一条 服务内容
本平台提供在线教育服务
版本 2.1 更新于 2025年3月

best_practices: Ensure source images have good contrast, sharp focus, and adequate resolution for optimal OCR accuracy
Select the correct language model in the configuration before running OCR on non-English text
Verify that Tesseract OCR is installed and accessible from the configured command path before execution
anti_patterns: Using low-resolution or heavily compressed images that produce unreliable OCR results
Running OCR without first confirming the required language pack is installed on the system
Expecting perfect recognition accuracy from degraded, handwritten, or poorly lit documents

faq.question: What image formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports PNG, JPEG, TIFF, BMP, GIF, and WebP image formats through the Pillow library.

faq.question: Do I need to install Tesseract OCR separately?
faq.answer: Yes, Tesseract OCR must be installed on your system. This skill uses pytesseract to call Tesseract, which is a separate system dependency.

faq.question: Can I use languages other than English?
faq.answer: Yes, set the lang configuration to any installed Tesseract language code such as chi_sim for Chinese, fra for French, or deu for German.

faq.question: How do I configure the Tesseract executable path?
faq.answer: Set the tesseract_cmd field in the JSON configuration file to the full path of the Tesseract executable if it is not on your system PATH.

faq.question: What should I do if OCR returns empty text?
faq.answer: Verify the image contains readable text, confirm the correct language model is selected, and check that Tesseract is properly installed.

faq.question: Can this skill process PDF files directly?
faq.answer: No, this skill processes image files only. PDF files must be converted to image format before OCR can be applied.
